channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026: $5,000 Scholarship Opportunity for Undergraduate Students in Illinois, Massachusetts, New York, Ohio, and Texas
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Students pursuing higher education often face significant financial challenges, especially when working toward degrees in competitive and high-demand fields. To help address these challenges and expand educational opportunities for deserving students, ORIX Corporation USA has launched the ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026. This scholarship program is designed to support students who are committed to […]

The post ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026: $5,000 Scholarship Opportunity for Undergraduate Students in Illinois, Massachusetts, New York, Ohio, and Texas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Students pursuing higher education often face significant financial challenges, especially when working toward degrees in competitive and high-demand fields. To help address these challenges and expand educational opportunities for deserving students, ORIX Corporation USA has launched the ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026.

This scholarship program is designed to support students who are committed to continuing their education at accredited four-year colleges and universities while pursuing studies in business-related, technology, and professional disciplines. Through this initiative, ORIX Corporation USA aims to invest in the next generation of leaders, innovators, and professionals by providing financial assistance to students who demonstrate academic achievement, leadership potential, and financial need.

The program is administered by Scholarship America, one of the largest and most respected scholarship management organizations in the United States. Scholarship America oversees the application review and recipient selection process independently, ensuring a fair and transparent evaluation of all eligible applicants.

Scholarship Overview

  • Scholarship Name: ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026
  • Sponsor: ORIX Corporation USA
  • Administrator: Scholarship America
  • Award Amount: $5,000
  • Number of Awards: Up to 4 scholarships
  • Study Level: Undergraduate
  • Institution Type: Accredited Four-Year Colleges and Universities
  • Application Deadline: July 8, 2026, at 3:00 PM Central Time (CT)
  • Notification Period: August 2026
  • Scholarship Payment Period: September 2026

Applicants should note that the application portal will close on July 8, 2026, or earlier if 200 applications are received before the deadline.

About the ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program

The ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program was established to provide financial assistance to students pursuing undergraduate education in specific fields that align with the company’s focus on business, finance, technology, and professional services.

The scholarship recognizes students who have demonstrated academic dedication, leadership skills, community involvement, and a commitment to achieving their educational and career goals despite financial or personal challenges.

By supporting students from underrepresented and economically disadvantaged backgrounds, the program contributes to educational equity and helps create pathways to long-term academic and professional success.

Eligible Fields of Study

Applicants must plan to pursue a degree in one of the following areas:

  • Finance
  • Accounting
  • Economics
  • Business Administration
  • Marketing
  • Communications
  • Technology
  • Real Estate
  • Human Resources

Students pursuing programs outside these fields are not eligible for the scholarship.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program, applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements:

Academic and Enrollment Requirements

Applicants must:

  1. Be a high school senior, high school graduate, or current undergraduate student.
  2. Plan to enroll in full-time undergraduate study for the entire upcoming academic year.
  3. Attend an accredited four-year college or university.
  4. Pursue one of the eligible fields of study listed above.
  5. Reside in or plan to attend college in one of the following states:
    • Illinois
    • Massachusetts
    • New York
    • Ohio
    • Texas

Additional Eligibility Criteria

In addition to the requirements above, applicants must meet at least one of the following conditions:

1. Low Socioeconomic Status

Applicants whose family income is at or below 150% of the current U.S. Department of Health and Human Services poverty guidelines for their household size may qualify.

2. Foster Care Status

Applicants who are currently in foster care or who have aged out of the foster care system are eligible to apply.

3. First-Generation College Student

Applicants may qualify if neither parent nor guardian has earned a bachelor’s degree from a four-year college or university.

Ineligible Applicants

The following individuals are not eligible:

  • Employees of ORIX USA
  • Spouses of employees
  • Dependents of employees
  • Members of employees’ households

Scholarship Benefits

Recipients selected for the ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program will receive:

  • A scholarship award of $5,000
  • Financial support toward undergraduate educational expenses
  • Recognition through a nationally administered scholarship program
  • An opportunity to reapply in future years if they continue to meet eligibility requirements

Although the scholarship is not renewable, previous recipients may submit new applications in future cycles if they remain eligible.

Selection Criteria

Scholarship recipients will be chosen through a competitive review process conducted by Scholarship America.

Selection will be based on:

  • Academic performance and achievement
  • Leadership experience
  • Participation in school activities
  • Community involvement and service
  • Work experience
  • Educational and career goals
  • Personal statement quality
  • Unusual personal or family circumstances
  • Demonstrated financial need

Applicants should ensure their submissions clearly highlight their achievements, experiences, aspirations, and challenges overcome.

Importantly, financial need is a major consideration in the selection process, and successful applicants must demonstrate a genuine need for financial assistance.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit all required materials electronically before the deadline.

1. Academic Transcript

A complete and current transcript must be uploaded and must include:

  • Student name
  • School name
  • Grades
  • Credit hours for each course
  • Academic terms during which courses were taken

Grade reports will not be accepted in place of official transcripts.

2. IRS Form 1040

Applicants must upload copies of the first two pages of their most recently filed IRS Form 1040 used for the financial information section of the application.

Before uploading, applicants should black out:

  • Social Security numbers
  • Bank account information

3. SAT or ACT Score Report (If Applicable)

Students providing SAT or ACT scores that are not included on their high school transcript must upload separate score reports.

Applicants submitting college transcripts are not required to provide SAT or ACT scores.

Important Application Notes

Applicants should carefully review all requirements before submitting their applications.

Key reminders include:

  • Applications are only considered complete when all required documents are submitted electronically.
  • Incomplete applications may not be reviewed.
  • Applicants should use a personal email address rather than a school or work email account.
  • Scholarship notifications will be sent via email.
  • The application portal may close before the deadline once 200 applications have been received.

Early submission is strongly encouraged.

Scholarship Timeline

Activity Date
Applications Open Currently Open
Application Deadline July 8, 2026, 3:00 PM CT
Application Closure Limit Earlier if 200 applications are received
Recipient Notification August 2026
Scholarship Payment September 2026

Why This Scholarship Matters

The ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program goes beyond financial assistance by helping students overcome barriers that may otherwise limit access to higher education. Through targeted support for first-generation college students, students from low-income households, and those with foster care experience, the scholarship seeks to create meaningful educational opportunities for individuals with strong academic and leadership potential.

For eligible students pursuing careers in finance, business, technology, communications, human resources, economics, accounting, or real estate, this scholarship provides valuable funding that can help reduce educational expenses and support long-term academic success.

Application Link

Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official Scholarship America scholarship portal before the deadline.

Deadline: July 8, 2026, at 3:00 PM Central Time (CT), or earlier if 200 applications are received.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post ORIX Corporation USA Scholars Program 2026: $5,000 Scholarship Opportunity for Undergraduate Students in Illinois, Massachusetts, New York, Ohio, and Texas appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the 2026–2027 World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council: Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Ocean and Climate Advocates
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Young leaders who are passionate about ocean conservation, climate action, environmental advocacy, and community impact now have an exciting opportunity to join the 2026–2027 World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council. Applications are officially open for young people from around the world who are already making a difference in their communities and are eager to strengthen […]

The post Apply for the 2026–2027 World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council: Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Ocean and Climate Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young leaders who are passionate about ocean conservation, climate action, environmental advocacy, and community impact now have an exciting opportunity to join the 2026–2027 World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council. Applications are officially open for young people from around the world who are already making a difference in their communities and are eager to strengthen their leadership skills, expand their global networks, and contribute to international efforts aimed at protecting our blue planet.

The World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council brings together a diverse group of 20–25 young leaders from different countries and backgrounds who are actively involved in conservation efforts. Since its establishment in 2016, the Council has played a significant role in advancing youth engagement and leadership within the global environmental movement.

About the World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council

The World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council is a global network of young environmental leaders committed to ocean protection, climate action, and sustainable development. Council members collaborate throughout the year to share ideas, strengthen conservation initiatives, develop leadership skills, and inspire collective action for a healthier planet.

The Council supports the broader mission of World Ocean Day, which seeks to unite individuals, organizations, businesses, governments, and communities worldwide in taking meaningful action for the ocean. Given that the ocean covers approximately 71% of the Earth’s surface and plays a critical role in regulating climate systems, the Council focuses on empowering young people to become influential voices in environmental stewardship.

Over the years, the Youth Advisory Council has grown into a vibrant global network. The Youth Advisory Council Alumni Network currently includes 173 members representing 75 countries, demonstrating the program’s international reach and impact.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Young people are increasingly at the forefront of environmental advocacy and climate action. The Youth Advisory Council provides participants with a unique platform to:

  • Collaborate with like-minded youth leaders from across the globe.
  • Develop practical leadership and advocacy skills.
  • Influence global conversations around ocean and climate protection.
  • Expand professional and conservation networks.
  • Gain visibility for their local environmental initiatives.
  • Access future opportunities through a worldwide network of organizations and conservation professionals.

Through this experience, participants can amplify their impact both locally and internationally.

What Council Members Will Do

Members of the Youth Advisory Council are expected to continue their existing conservation work while contributing to broader global initiatives.

Key responsibilities include:

1. Deepen Community Impact

Council members will continue the conservation, advocacy, and environmental initiatives they are already leading in their communities while exploring ways to increase their effectiveness and reach.

2. Participate in Conservation Events

Members are encouraged to engage in ocean, climate, and environmental events throughout the year at local, national, regional, and international levels.

3. Serve as World Ocean Day Ambassadors

Council members act as ambassadors for World Ocean Day by promoting awareness and encouraging individuals and organizations to take action for ocean protection.

4. Collaborate with Global Peers

Participants work closely with fellow Council members and alumni on projects such as:

  • Organizing events
  • Developing educational resources
  • Supporting conservation campaigns
  • Creating community engagement initiatives
  • Promoting youth-led environmental action

5. Provide Strategic Guidance

Members contribute ideas and perspectives that help shape the future direction of World Ocean Day and related conservation campaigns.

6. Participate in Public Awareness Campaigns

Council members may:

  • Share environmental content through social media.
  • Contribute blog posts highlighting community projects.
  • Participate in social media takeovers.
  • Engage in awareness and outreach campaigns.

Program Commitment

Applicants should be prepared to actively participate throughout the one-year term.

Commitment requirements include:

  • One-year term lasting through June 2027.
  • Attendance at a minimum of five virtual video conference meetings.
  • Completion of three to four advisory surveys during the term.
  • Regular communication through email and WhatsApp at least once or twice each month.
  • Participation in the Youth Leadership Training Program.
  • Engagement in regional hub development activities.
  • Optional participation in collaborative projects with other Council members.

Youth Leadership Training Program

A major benefit of the Council is access to a comprehensive Youth Leadership Training Program.

The training includes:

  • Virtual leadership sessions.
  • Weekly one-hour meetings.
  • Additional 30-minute discussion sessions exclusively for Council members.
  • Four-week training period scheduled for October/November 2026 (subject to change).

The program is designed to strengthen participants’ abilities in:

  • Campaign organization
  • Advocacy
  • Community mobilization
  • Event planning
  • Goal setting and implementation
  • Public engagement
  • Environmental leadership

Benefits of Joining the Youth Advisory Council

Successful applicants gain access to a wide range of personal, professional, and leadership development opportunities.

Benefits include:

Leadership Development

  • Comprehensive onboarding.
  • Professional leadership training.
  • Practical advocacy experience.

Global Recognition

Council members receive opportunities to showcase their work through:

  • Guest blog posts.
  • Social media features.
  • Newsletter spotlights.
  • Media interviews.
  • Speaking engagements.

International Networking

Participants can connect with:

  • Current Council members.
  • Alumni across 75 countries.
  • Environmental organizations.
  • Conservation professionals.
  • Policymakers and advocates.

Event and Campaign Experience

Members receive hands-on opportunities to:

  • Organize environmental events.
  • Lead campaigns.
  • Coordinate community initiatives.
  • Mobilize public participation.

Access to Future Opportunities

The Ocean Project works with a global network of more than 2,000 organizations in nearly 200 countries. Council members may gain access to future opportunities including:

  • Internships
  • Professional positions
  • Collaborative projects
  • Conference participation
  • Environmental leadership initiatives

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be between 16 and 25 years old at the time of application.
  • Be fluent in English.
  • Have reliable internet access.
  • Demonstrate previous conservation experience.
  • Show commitment to environmental protection and climate action.

Advocacy and organizing experience are considered an added advantage but are not mandatory.

Ideal Candidates

The program is seeking young people who:

  • Are already engaged in conservation activities.
  • Understand the connection between ocean health and climate stability.
  • Value collaboration and collective action.
  • Want to share ideas and experiences with a global audience.
  • Are eager to build leadership and advocacy skills.
  • Are committed to environmental restoration and sustainability.

Application Process

The application process follows several stages:

Stage 1: Initial Application

Applications opened on 25 June 2026.

Stage 2: Application Deadline

Deadline: 2 August 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline.

Stage 3: Second-Round Notification

Candidates selected for the next stage will be notified on 10 August 2026.

Stage 4: Recommendation Letters

Second-round applicants will be required to provide recommendation letters from:

  • Teachers
  • Advisors
  • Employers
  • Other non-parent mentors

Stage 5: Final Selection

New Council members will be notified by late September 2026.

About The Ocean Project

The Ocean Project coordinates World Ocean Day globally and works to promote collective action for healthy oceans and a stable climate. Since launching global coordination efforts in 2002, the organization has collaborated with youth leaders, community organizations, businesses, and conservation partners worldwide.

Through partnerships spanning nearly 200 countries, The Ocean Project continues to strengthen public engagement, support environmental education, and encourage meaningful action to protect marine ecosystems and address climate challenges.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their application through the official World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council application portal.

Application Deadline: 2 August 2026

Early applications are strongly encouraged to maximize preparation time for subsequent application stages.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Apply for the 2026–2027 World Ocean Day Youth Advisory Council: Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Ocean and Climate Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Pathway for Emerging Development Economics Researchers from Low and Lower-Middle-Income Countries
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship 2027 is a prestigious opportunity designed to identify, support, and elevate talented aspiring researchers from low and lower-middle-income countries who are interested in pursuing academic careers in development economics. Offered by the Weiss Fund for Research in Development Economics, this fellowship aims to bridge the gap between undergraduate or master’s […]

The post Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Pathway for Emerging Development Economics Researchers from Low and Lower-Middle-Income Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship 2027 is a prestigious opportunity designed to identify, support, and elevate talented aspiring researchers from low and lower-middle-income countries who are interested in pursuing academic careers in development economics. Offered by the Weiss Fund for Research in Development Economics, this fellowship aims to bridge the gap between undergraduate or master’s studies and doctoral-level education by connecting promising candidates with leading economists and world-class universities.

The fellowship seeks to address the underrepresentation of researchers from developing countries within highly competitive predoctoral programs. Through endorsement, mentorship opportunities, and institutional support, successful applicants gain access to pathways that can significantly strengthen their prospects of securing admission into top-tier Ph.D. programs in Economics.

About the Weiss Fund for Research in Development Economics

The Weiss Fund for Research in Development Economics supports innovative and impactful research that improves the lives of people living in developing countries. The fund is committed to fostering rigorous, evidence-based solutions to complex development challenges while nurturing the next generation of researchers in the field.

Beyond funding research projects, the Weiss Fund actively works to:

  • Attract talented individuals into development economics.
  • Support creative and rigorous academic research.
  • Build stronger connections between research, policy, and implementation.
  • Expand opportunities for talented researchers from underrepresented regions.
  • Promote equitable access to academic mentorship and research training.

Through the Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship, the Weiss Fund continues this mission by helping talented candidates gain access to leading research environments and mentorship from renowned development economists.

What Is the Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship?

The fellowship is specifically designed for individuals interested in pursuing predoctoral research positions. Predoctoral roles serve as an important stepping stone between undergraduate education and doctoral studies, providing hands-on research experience, advanced quantitative training, and mentorship from experienced academics.

Successful fellowship finalists may receive:

  • Official endorsement from the Weiss Fund.
  • Opportunities to be invited by leading faculty members to apply for pre-approved two-year predoctoral research positions.
  • Increased visibility among top universities and development economists.
  • Support throughout the placement process.
  • Access to valuable academic and professional networks.

Previous fellowship finalists have secured positions with Weiss Fund-affiliated faculty at globally recognized institutions including:

  • Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
  • Northwestern University
  • Stanford University
  • University of California, Berkeley
  • Yale University

While placements vary each year, finalists may be invited to apply for opportunities at leading universities participating in the fellowship network.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Many talented students from developing countries face barriers when attempting to enter elite academic and research pathways. These barriers often include:

  • Limited access to mentorship.
  • Lack of exposure to internationally recognized economists.
  • Fewer opportunities for research experience.
  • Limited access to recommendation networks.
  • Financial constraints associated with academic advancement.

The Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship is specifically designed to reduce these barriers and create pathways for highly talented researchers who demonstrate strong potential for success in development economics.

Benefits of the Fellowship

Successful finalists benefit from several advantages throughout the application and placement process.

1. Endorsement for Highly Competitive Predoctoral Programs

The Weiss Fund endorsement provides candidates with a valuable signal of academic potential and research capability. Participating universities take this endorsement into consideration during their selection process.

2. Opportunity to Work with Leading Economists

Fellows who secure placement may work directly with globally recognized development economists on cutting-edge research projects.

3. Research and Technical Skill Development

Participants may strengthen their:

  • Quantitative analysis skills.
  • Coding and programming abilities.
  • Research methodology knowledge.
  • Data analysis competencies.
  • Academic writing skills.

4. Exposure to Real-World Research

Predoctoral positions provide practical experience through:

  • Research project management.
  • Fieldwork exposure.
  • Data collection and analysis.
  • Policy-relevant research activities.
  • Research publication support.

5. Mentorship for Doctoral Applications

Participants receive guidance that can significantly improve future Ph.D. applications through:

  • Academic mentorship.
  • Graduate school preparation.
  • Research career advice.
  • Professional development opportunities.

6. Publication and Collaboration Opportunities

Fellows may have opportunities to:

  • Collaborate on academic publications.
  • Contribute to high-impact research projects.
  • Potentially co-author research papers.

7. Increased Academic Visibility

Finalists and fellowship recipients may be featured on the Weiss Fund website, providing visibility to their academic interests, achievements, and research potential.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the fellowship, applicants must satisfy the following requirements:

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • A Bachelor’s degree or Master’s degree from an institution located in a low-income or lower-middle-income country.

Alternatively:

  • Applicants who completed secondary education in a low-income or lower-middle-income country but obtained their university education elsewhere may be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Academic Requirements

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong mathematical abilities.
  • Strong quantitative reasoning skills.
  • Proficiency in written and spoken English.

GRE Requirement

Applicants are expected to have:

  • A GRE Quantitative score above 160.

Candidates who have not yet taken the GRE may still apply under the designated application track and may later be required to complete a similar assessment administered by the Weiss Fund.

Preferred Qualifications

Competitive candidates may possess one or more of the following qualifications.

Academic Background

Degrees in fields such as:

  • Economics
  • Mathematics
  • Statistics
  • Physics
  • Engineering
  • Computer Science
  • Actuarial Science
  • Related social science disciplines with strong quantitative foundations

Research Experience

Preferred applicants may have experience in:

  • Academic research environments.
  • Data analysis projects.
  • Quantitative research.
  • Development economics research.
  • Randomized Controlled Trials (RCTs).

Technical Skills

Experience with statistical and programming software is highly valued, including:

  • Stata
  • R
  • Python
  • Matlab

Professional Competencies

Strong candidates typically demonstrate:

  • Interest in development economics research.
  • Commitment to continuous learning.
  • Ability to work independently.
  • Strong teamwork skills.
  • Excellent communication abilities.
  • Effective time-management skills.
  • Attention to detail.
  • Ability to thrive in fast-paced environments.

GRE Preferences for Advanced Selection Stages

During later phases of the selection process, stronger consideration may be given to candidates with:

  • GRE Quantitative score above 168 or 90th percentile.
  • GRE Verbal score of 156 or above (70th percentile).

The Weiss Fund will cover GRE examination fees for selected pre-finalists who have not yet completed the test.

Fellowship Award Terms

Successful applicants may be matched with host institutions following independent evaluation processes conducted by participating universities.

Important conditions include:

  • Host institutions will handle visa sponsorship.
  • Host institutions will provide compensation and relevant employment benefits.
  • The Weiss Fund will subsidize part of the hiring costs.
  • Finalists may receive invitations to apply to one or more pre-approved predoctoral positions.

Application Tracks

Applicants may choose from two application pathways.

Track 1: GRE Score Available

Applicants who already possess GRE scores can:

  • Apply by July 24, 2026.
  • Be reviewed on a rolling basis.
  • Potentially be considered for placement beginning Fall 2026.

If GRE scores do not meet the required threshold, applicants may be asked to retake the examination, with costs covered by the Weiss Fund.

Track 2: GRE Score Pending

Applicants without GRE scores may:

  • Submit applications by July 24, 2026.
  • Receive additional preparation time.
  • Complete the GRE after initial screening.

Final decisions for this track are expected between December 2026 and January 2027.

The Weiss Fund will cover GRE fees for eligible pre-finalists.

Application Deadline

Deadline: July 24, 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their complete applications before the deadline.

Application Process

The application process includes the following stages:

  1. Submit a complete application by July 24, 2026.
  2. Undergo initial review and screening.
  3. Receive invitation for further assessment if shortlisted.
  4. Complete GRE requirements if necessary.
  5. Participate in final selection and placement coordination.
  6. Potentially receive invitations to apply for pre-approved predoctoral positions.

Applicants should note that placement and visa processing may take several months after selection.

Required Documents

Applicants must prepare the following:

  • Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  • Academic transcripts.
  • GRE quantitative score (if available).

Recommendation letters are not required during the initial application stage but will be requested from candidates who advance further in the selection process.

Final Thoughts

The Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship 2027 represents an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers from low and lower-middle-income countries to access world-class mentorship, advanced research training, and highly competitive predoctoral opportunities. Through endorsement from the Weiss Fund and potential placement with leading economists at top universities, successful applicants can significantly strengthen their academic profiles and prepare for future doctoral studies in Economics.

For talented individuals seeking to build a career in development economics, this fellowship provides a valuable pathway toward academic excellence and global research impact.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Kabir Banerjee Predoctoral Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Pathway for Emerging Development Economics Researchers from Low and Lower-Middle-Income Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Union Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) Recruitment 2026 in Ghana | Earn Up to US$77,425 Per Year
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The African Union (AU) has officially opened applications for the Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) position at the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) Secretariat in Accra, Ghana. This exciting international career opportunity is ideal for young professionals seeking to contribute to Africa’s economic integration while building a rewarding career within one of the continent’s most […]

The post African Union Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) Recruitment 2026 in Ghana | Earn Up to US$77,425 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Union (AU) has officially opened applications for the Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) position at the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) Secretariat in Accra, Ghana. This exciting international career opportunity is ideal for young professionals seeking to contribute to Africa’s economic integration while building a rewarding career within one of the continent’s most respected intergovernmental organizations.

The position offers competitive remuneration, career development opportunities, exposure to high-level regional programmes, and the chance to work directly on initiatives supporting Africa’s largest free trade agreement.

If you possess experience in monitoring and evaluation, programme coordination, economics, statistics, development studies, public finance, or related disciplines, this could be your opportunity to join the African Union and contribute to the successful implementation of Agenda 2063.

About the African Union

The African Union (AU) is Africa’s premier continental organization dedicated to promoting unity, peace, sustainable development, economic integration, and prosperity among its member states.

Through Agenda 2063, the AU seeks to create:

  • An integrated Africa
  • A prosperous Africa
  • A peaceful Africa
  • A citizen-driven Africa
  • A globally influential Africa

The African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) is one of the flagship projects under Agenda 2063 and represents one of the world’s largest free trade areas.

Position Overview

Position: Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA)

Requisition ID: 3020

Location: Accra, Ghana

Department: Director Coordination & Programmes

Reports To: Program Officer

Job Grade: P1

Contract Type: Fixed-Term

The successful candidate will support programme coordination, implementation, monitoring, and resource mobilization activities that strengthen funding coordination and ensure effective implementation of AfCFTA programmes.

Purpose of the Position

The Junior Program Officer will assist in coordinating and monitoring resource mobilization initiatives that support programme implementation across the AfCFTA Secretariat.

The role focuses on ensuring:

  • Better coordination among programmes.
  • Effective implementation of funding plans.
  • Improved partnerships.
  • Enhanced monitoring.
  • Reduced duplication of efforts.
  • Sustainable programme financing.

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will perform a wide range of responsibilities, including:

Programme Coordination

  • Monitor implementation of resource mobilization work plans.
  • Track programme progress and prepare reports.
  • Support implementation of funding strategies.
  • Coordinate programme activities across departments.

Resource Mobilization

  • Review existing resource mobilization frameworks.
  • Support continuous improvement of funding strategies.
  • Assist departments in updating resource mobilization plans.
  • Facilitate financing discussions with stakeholders.

Monitoring and Evaluation

  • Work closely with Monitoring and Evaluation teams.
  • Ensure implementation follows established standards.
  • Support performance tracking.
  • Promote accountability throughout programme implementation.

Coordination and Partnerships

  • Promote information sharing across departments.
  • Identify and reduce duplication of efforts.
  • Strengthen collaboration among programme teams.
  • Maintain productive working relationships with stakeholders.

Capacity Building

  • Support capacity development for AU Member States.
  • Assist in organizing structured financing dialogues.
  • Strengthen institutional knowledge on resource mobilization.

Additional Duties

The officer may also perform other responsibilities assigned by supervisors or the Director.

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess a university degree in one of the following fields:

  • Monitoring and Evaluation
  • Economics
  • Statistics
  • Development Studies
  • Demography
  • Public Finance
  • Or another related discipline

Professional Experience

Candidates must have:

  • At least two (2) years of relevant professional experience.

Experience in programme management, monitoring, evaluation, donor coordination, development finance, or public sector programmes will be advantageous.

Required Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Excellent written communication skills.
  • Strong verbal communication abilities.
  • Sound planning and organizational skills.
  • Ability to manage uncertainty.
  • Cultural sensitivity.
  • Ability to work across departments and countries.
  • Strong teamwork skills.
  • Good analytical abilities.

Leadership Competencies

Successful applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strategic insight
  • Developing others
  • Change management
  • Risk management

Core Competencies

The African Union values candidates who exhibit:

  • Teamwork and collaboration
  • Accountability
  • Compliance with organizational rules
  • Continuous learning
  • Professional ethics

Functional Competencies

Candidates should possess:

  • Strong influencing and communication skills.
  • Technical job knowledge.
  • Information sharing abilities.
  • Results-oriented mindset.
  • Continuous improvement focus.

Contract Duration

The appointment will initially be offered on a fixed-term contract of one or two years.

The first six months will serve as a probation period.

Following successful performance evaluation and subject to funding availability, the contract may be renewed up to two additional times.

Salary and Benefits

The African Union offers an attractive remuneration package.

Annual salary includes:

  • US$77,425.50 for internationally recruited staff.
  • US$59,805.19 for locally recruited staff.

These amounts are inclusive of applicable allowances.

Language Requirements

Applicants must be proficient in at least one of the African Union’s official working languages:

  • Arabic
  • English
  • French
  • Kiswahili
  • Portuguese
  • Spanish

Knowledge of an additional AU language is considered an added advantage.

Equal Opportunity Employer

The African Union is committed to equal employment opportunities.

Qualified women are strongly encouraged to apply.

The AU also encourages applications from underrepresented member states to promote balanced continental representation.

Required Application Documents

Applicants should prepare:

  • A fully completed online application.
  • The mandatory African Union CV template.
  • An African passport.
  • Academic certificates and qualifications.
  • Relevant professional certificates.

Failure to use the mandatory AU CV template may affect the application.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the online application through the African Union Careers Portal.

Before applying:

  1. Download the official AU CV template.
  2. Complete the template accurately.
  3. Upload all required supporting documents.
  4. Submit the application before the deadline.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted no later than 7 July 2026 at 11:59 p.m. UTC.

Late submissions will not be considered.

Apply Here

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity provides professionals with the chance to:

  • Work with Africa’s leading continental organization.
  • Contribute to implementation of the AfCFTA.
  • Build international development experience.
  • Earn a competitive salary.
  • Develop expertise in programme coordination and resource mobilization.
  • Collaborate with governments, development partners, and regional institutions.
  • Advance a meaningful career supporting Africa’s economic transformation.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post African Union Junior Program Officer (AfCFTA) Recruitment 2026 in Ghana | Earn Up to US$77,425 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nexa Climate and Health Innovation Funding 2026: Up to Transform Local Health Systems Across Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Climate change is no longer only an environmental issue—it has become one of the greatest public health challenges of our time. Rising temperatures, changing rainfall patterns, poor air quality, and the rapid spread of mosquito-borne diseases are placing unprecedented pressure on healthcare systems, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. To help address these growing challenges, […]

The post Nexa Climate and Health Innovation Funding 2026: Up to Transform Local Health Systems Across Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Climate change is no longer only an environmental issue—it has become one of the greatest public health challenges of our time. Rising temperatures, changing rainfall patterns, poor air quality, and the rapid spread of mosquito-borne diseases are placing unprecedented pressure on healthcare systems, particularly in low- and middle-income countries.

To help address these growing challenges, Nexa, a global climate and health innovation initiative, is investing in bold, locally led innovations that enable health systems to anticipate climate-related risks and respond before they become health emergencies.

Led by Grand Challenges Canada and the Science for Africa Foundation, together with an international consortium of funding partners, Nexa is now accepting applications for its 2026 Climate and Health Innovation Funding Opportunity.

Organizations and innovators from Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean are invited to submit innovative solutions that strengthen healthcare systems and protect vulnerable communities from climate-driven health threats.

Application Deadline: July 22, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. Eastern Time (ET) / 6:00 p.m. UTC

About Nexa

Nexa is a global initiative dedicated to accelerating climate and health innovations that improve the resilience of health systems facing increasing climate-related challenges.

Rather than applying one-size-fits-all approaches, Nexa prioritizes locally led innovations, recognizing that health workers, researchers, innovators, and humanitarian organizations working directly within affected communities understand their challenges better than anyone else.

The initiative focuses on turning climate-risk information into practical health actions that reduce illness and save lives.

Nexa is co-led by:

  • Grand Challenges Canada
  • Science for Africa Foundation

Working alongside global partners including:

  • Novo Nordisk Foundation
  • Sanofi Foundation
  • Fund for Innovation in Development
  • Global Grand Challenges Network
  • Botswana Innovation Fund
  • NCST Malawi
  • NCST Rwanda
  • Institut Pasteur Dakar
  • South African Medical Research Council
  • Government of Canada

Why Climate and Health Innovation Matters

Climate change is creating new health emergencies across the world.

Approximately 3.6 billion people currently live in regions that are highly vulnerable to climate change.

The effects are becoming increasingly severe, including:

  • Expansion of mosquito-borne diseases into new regions.
  • Increased frequency of extreme heat events.
  • Poor air quality affecting millions of people.
  • Greater pressure on already overstretched health systems.

Communities that experience the greatest risks include:

  • Pregnant women
  • Children
  • Older adults
  • People living with chronic illnesses
  • Rural and underserved populations
  • Communities with limited healthcare access

Without stronger healthcare systems capable of anticipating these risks, millions of people remain vulnerable.

Community-Led Innovation Drives Nexa

Unlike many global funding initiatives, Nexa’s priorities have been shaped directly by frontline professionals.

In January 2025, Grand Challenges Canada, the Science for Africa Foundation, the Global Grand Challenges Network, and The Geneva Learning Foundation launched the largest global climate and health survey ever conducted.

The survey gathered responses from:

  • More than 6,400 health and humanitarian workers
  • Across 107 low- and middle-income countries

These insights helped identify the most urgent climate-health challenges and informed Nexa’s investment priorities.

Nexa’s Areas of Focus

Applicants should develop innovations that address one or more of the following priority areas.

1. Climate-Informed Early Warning and Monitoring Systems

Nexa seeks innovations that help communities detect climate-related health risks before they escalate into emergencies.

Examples include:

  • Early warning systems
  • Climate surveillance platforms
  • Disease forecasting tools
  • Integrated weather and health monitoring systems
  • Risk prediction technologies
  • Community alert systems

Solutions should help communities prepare for:

  • Mosquito-borne diseases
  • Extreme heat
  • Air pollution
  • Other climate-driven health risks

2. Climate-Responsive Patient Care Delivery

Healthcare providers need better tools to continue delivering quality care during climate emergencies.

Nexa supports innovations that improve:

  • Diagnosis
  • Treatment
  • Patient monitoring
  • Continuity of healthcare services
  • Health service delivery during climate stress

Projects may focus on:

  • Heat-related illnesses
  • Air pollution-related diseases
  • Malaria
  • Dengue
  • Other mosquito-borne infections

What Kind of Innovations Does Nexa Support?

Successful proposals may include innovations that:

  • Combine weather data with health information.
  • Improve disease prediction.
  • Support healthcare decision-making.
  • Enable rapid response before disease outbreaks occur.
  • Improve patient care during climate events.
  • Help healthcare systems remain operational during climate stress.
  • Strengthen local healthcare capacity.
  • Reduce climate-related illness and deaths.

Nexa particularly encourages practical, scalable, and community-driven solutions.

Why This Funding Opportunity Is Important

Climate change affects countries differently.

Local innovators understand local realities better than external organizations.

Nexa therefore prioritizes:

  • Local leadership
  • Community ownership
  • Evidence-based innovation
  • Sustainable health solutions
  • Scalable technologies
  • Strong partnerships

The initiative seeks solutions that can create lasting improvements while protecting the most vulnerable populations.

Who Should Apply?

This funding opportunity is ideal for:

  • Health innovators
  • Universities
  • Research institutions
  • Technology developers
  • Public health organizations
  • NGOs
  • Healthcare providers
  • Social enterprises
  • Climate innovators
  • Humanitarian organizations

Applicants should have innovative ideas capable of strengthening healthcare delivery in climate-vulnerable settings.

Benefits of the Nexa Climate and Health Innovation Funding

Selected innovators will benefit from:

  • Funding to advance innovative climate-health solutions.
  • Opportunities to scale impactful innovations.
  • Global visibility.
  • Collaboration with leading international partners.
  • Access to an international climate-health innovation network.
  • Support for strengthening local healthcare systems.
  • Contribution to improving health outcomes in climate-vulnerable communities.

Important Deadline

Applications must be submitted by July 22, 2026, at 2:00 p.m. ET (6:00 p.m. UTC).

Late applications may not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should review the complete funding guidelines and application requirements before submitting their proposals.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and ensure their projects clearly demonstrate how they will strengthen climate resilience within healthcare systems.

 Apply Here

Final Thoughts

Climate change is rapidly transforming global health challenges, demanding innovative and locally driven responses. Through Nexa, innovators across Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean have an opportunity to develop groundbreaking solutions that help healthcare systems anticipate climate risks, protect vulnerable communities, and improve health outcomes for millions of people.

Organizations with bold ideas that combine climate science, healthcare, technology, and community engagement are strongly encouraged to apply before the application deadline.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Nexa Climate and Health Innovation Funding 2026: Up to Transform Local Health Systems Across Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MFWA 2026 Fully Funded Training Workshop for Journalists in Anglophone West Africa: Apply to Strengthen Your Reporting on Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA), in partnership with the Wits Centre for Journalism (WCJ) in South Africa, has officially opened applications for its 2026 Training Workshop on Critical Coverage of Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations. This fully funded capacity-building programme is designed for journalists from selected Anglophone West African countries who are […]

The post MFWA 2026 Fully Funded Training Workshop for Journalists in Anglophone West Africa: Apply to Strengthen Your Reporting on Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Media Foundation for West Africa (MFWA), in partnership with the Wits Centre for Journalism (WCJ) in South Africa, has officially opened applications for its 2026 Training Workshop on Critical Coverage of Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations. This fully funded capacity-building programme is designed for journalists from selected Anglophone West African countries who are interested in strengthening their reporting on one of the most important global relationships shaping Africa’s future.

Scheduled to take place in Accra, Ghana, during the last quarter of 2026, the three-day workshop will bring together journalists from across the region to deepen their understanding of the complex political, economic, and social dimensions of Africa–China relations. The organisers aim to equip media professionals with the knowledge, analytical skills, and investigative techniques required to produce balanced, evidence-based, and impactful journalism that serves the public interest.

Application Deadline: July 30, 2026

About the MFWA Training Workshop

Africa’s relationship with China has expanded significantly over the past two decades. China has become one of Africa’s largest trading partners and investors, financing major infrastructure projects, supporting industrial development, and increasing cooperation in technology, mining, agriculture, energy, and manufacturing.

However, this growing relationship also raises important questions surrounding:

  • Trade and economic partnerships
  • Infrastructure development
  • Development financing
  • Natural resource extraction
  • Labour practices
  • Technology transfer
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Governance and accountability
  • International diplomacy
  • Public policy

As geopolitical dynamics continue to evolve, journalists play a critical role in helping citizens understand the opportunities, challenges, and implications of Africa–China cooperation. Reporting on these issues requires more than simply covering official announcements—it demands context, critical analysis, investigative skills, and an understanding of international affairs.

Recognising this need, the Media Foundation for West Africa has partnered with the Wits Centre for Journalism to organise this specialised workshop titled “Building Capacity to Cover a Complex Story – Africa and China Relations.”

The programme is funded by the University of the Witwatersrand and is intended to strengthen the reporting capacity of journalists across Anglophone West Africa.

What Participants Will Learn

The workshop combines subject-matter expertise with practical journalism skills. Participants will gain insights into:

  • Understanding the evolving Africa–China relationship
  • Reporting on international trade and investment
  • Analysing development financing and infrastructure projects
  • Investigating governance and accountability issues
  • Covering natural resource extraction responsibly
  • Reporting on labour, technology, and environmental concerns
  • Applying investigative journalism techniques
  • Producing balanced, accurate, and evidence-based stories
  • Framing stories from African perspectives
  • Understanding global geopolitical shifts and their implications for African countries

The training seeks to improve both the depth and quality of reporting on international affairs while encouraging journalism that promotes transparency and informed public debate.

Benefits of the Programme

Successful applicants will receive comprehensive financial support throughout the programme.

The organisers will cover:

  • Return economy-class airfare
  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Local transportation within Ghana

This ensures participants can focus entirely on learning and networking without worrying about travel expenses.

Additional Opportunities After the Training

One of the most exciting aspects of this programme is that participation does not end after the workshop.

Graduates will have the opportunity to apply for:

  • Reporting grants to produce in-depth investigative stories on Africa–China relations.
  • Funding support for publishing high-quality public-interest journalism.
  • Professional mentorship and continued engagement with experts.

Furthermore, three outstanding participants will be selected for a fully funded opportunity to attend the Africa Investigative Journalism Conference (AIJC), scheduled for 10–12 November 2026 in Kenya, where they will present and discuss their reporting projects.

Who Can Apply?

Applicants must meet all of the following requirements:

  • Be based in one of the eligible Anglophone West African countries.
  • Work in print, radio, television, or digital journalism.
  • Report on one or more of the following beats:
    • Politics
    • Economy
    • Business
    • Trade
    • Development
    • Environment
    • Extractive industries
    • Infrastructure
    • International affairs
  • Be available during September–October 2026.
  • Obtain a formal permission letter from their editor allowing participation.
  • Be capable of producing an in-depth story after completing the workshop.

Why Journalists Should Apply

Today’s journalists increasingly cover stories that extend beyond national borders. International trade agreements, foreign investments, infrastructure financing, and geopolitical developments directly affect local communities and national development.

This workshop provides a unique opportunity to strengthen professional skills while learning from leading experts in journalism and international affairs.

Participants will also build valuable regional networks with fellow journalists, exchange experiences, and gain practical tools for producing investigative stories that inform public debate and promote accountability.

For journalists interested in politics, economics, business reporting, development journalism, or investigative reporting, this programme represents an important opportunity for career development and professional growth.

How to Apply

Interested journalists should complete the official online application form provided by the Media Foundation for West Africa. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications carefully and ensure they meet every eligibility requirement before submitting.

Application Deadline: July 30, 2026.

For more information and to submit your application, visit the official MFWA application page:

Discover more global workshop opportunities on OFY here

The post MFWA 2026 Fully Funded Training Workshop for Journalists in Anglophone West Africa: Apply to Strengthen Your Reporting on Africa–China Trade, Investment and Political Relations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Researchers making groundbreaking contributions to the fight against cancer now have the opportunity to gain one of the world’s most prestigious recognitions in oncology. The Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 has officially opened applications, inviting outstanding cancer researchers from across the globe to compete for an award valued at €200,000. Established by the Fondation Gustave Roussy, […]

The post Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers making groundbreaking contributions to the fight against cancer now have the opportunity to gain one of the world’s most prestigious recognitions in oncology. The Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 has officially opened applications, inviting outstanding cancer researchers from across the globe to compete for an award valued at €200,000.

Established by the Fondation Gustave Roussy, with the support of Laboratoires Pierre Fabre as its founding sponsor, the prize recognizes researchers whose scientific discoveries have significantly transformed cancer diagnosis, treatment, prevention, or patient care. Since its launch in 2025, the award has rapidly become one of the most highly respected international distinctions in oncology research, celebrating scientific excellence and innovations that deliver measurable and lasting benefits for cancer patients worldwide.

Application Deadline: August 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. CEST

About the Gustave Roussy Prize

Cancer remains one of the world’s leading health challenges, affecting millions of people every year. Continuous scientific research has resulted in remarkable progress in early diagnosis, precision medicine, immunotherapy, targeted therapies, prevention strategies, and patient-centered care. The Gustave Roussy Prize was created to recognize researchers whose discoveries have contributed substantially to these advances.

The award was launched in 2025 to commemorate two important milestones:

  • The 150th anniversary of Professor Gustave Roussy’s birth.
  • The centenary of the Gustave Roussy Institute.

Professor Gustave Roussy is widely recognized as a pioneer in cancer care. In 1921, he established one of the world’s first medical consultations dedicated specifically to patients with tumors, laying the foundation for modern comprehensive oncology care. Today, Gustave Roussy is recognized as Europe’s leading cancer center and continues to drive innovation through cutting-edge research, education, and patient care.

Prize Value

The Gustave Roussy Prize carries a total value of €200,000, making it one of the largest international awards dedicated to cancer research.

The funding is divided into two components:

  1. €100,000 awarded directly to the laureate for unrestricted personal use.
  2. €100,000 allocated to support an innovative oncology research project, enabling continued scientific discovery and future breakthroughs.

Purpose of the Award

The prize celebrates scientific breakthroughs that have created tangible improvements in cancer care and patient outcomes.

Research achievements may relate to:

  • Cancer diagnosis
  • Cancer treatment
  • Cancer prevention
  • Clinical oncology
  • Translational medicine
  • Precision medicine
  • Immunotherapy
  • Biomarker discovery
  • Screening technologies
  • Therapeutic innovation
  • Improved quality of patient care
  • Better understanding of cancer biology

The emphasis is not simply on academic achievement but on research that demonstrates measurable clinical impact and has improved the lives of patients.

Who Can Apply?

Applications are welcomed from researchers worldwide regardless of nationality or institutional affiliation.

Eligible candidates include researchers working at:

  • Universities
  • Cancer research institutes
  • Medical schools
  • Hospitals
  • Public research organizations
  • Private research institutions
  • International research centers

Applicants are expected to demonstrate exceptional scientific excellence and a research record that has significantly advanced oncology.

Selection Process

Applications will be evaluated by an independent international jury composed of leading experts in oncology and biomedical research.

Selection will be based exclusively on:

  • Scientific excellence
  • Originality of discoveries
  • Clinical impact
  • Innovation
  • Long-term contribution to oncology
  • Demonstrated benefits for patients

Importantly, evaluations are conducted without regard to discipline, institution, or nationality, ensuring a fair and merit-based selection process.

Why This Prize Matters

Receiving the Gustave Roussy Prize represents more than financial recognition. It places recipients among the world’s leading oncology researchers and highlights discoveries that have changed the landscape of cancer care.

The prize also encourages continued innovation by providing dedicated funding for future research projects, ensuring that today’s breakthroughs continue generating tomorrow’s solutions.

With cancer affecting millions globally, supporting transformative research remains essential for improving survival rates, enhancing quality of life, and developing more effective treatments. This award reinforces the importance of scientific collaboration and recognizes researchers whose work creates lasting benefits for humanity.

Previous Laureates

The prize has already honored internationally renowned scientists.

Previous recipients include:

  • 2025: Professor Charles Swanton
  • 2026: Professor Luis Diaz Jr.

These researchers were recognized for discoveries that have significantly influenced modern oncology and patient treatment.

Award Ceremony

The selected laureate for the 2027 edition will receive the prize on February 4, 2027, during World Cancer Day, highlighting the global importance of cancer research and international collaboration in combating the disease.

How to Apply

Researchers interested in the Gustave Roussy Prize 2027 should complete the official online application before the deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to present their most impactful scientific achievements, clearly demonstrate clinical relevance, and provide evidence showing how their discoveries have advanced cancer diagnosis, treatment, prevention, or patient care.

Application Deadline: August 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. CEST.

Interested applicants can find more information through the official Gustave Roussy website

Discover more global awards opportunities on OFY here

 

The post Gustave Roussy Prize 2027: €200,000 International Cancer Research Award Now Accepting Applications Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Aspen Institute’s Religion & Society Program is now accepting applications for the Michael Cromartie Forum 2026, an exclusive professional development opportunity designed for emerging journalists who want to deepen their understanding of the role religion plays in shaping politics, culture, public policy, and civic life. Taking place in Middleburg, Virginia, USA, from August 30 […]

The post Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Aspen Institute’s Religion & Society Program is now accepting applications for the Michael Cromartie Forum 2026, an exclusive professional development opportunity designed for emerging journalists who want to deepen their understanding of the role religion plays in shaping politics, culture, public policy, and civic life.

Taking place in Middleburg, Virginia, USA, from August 30 to September 1, 2026, the Forum provides an intellectually engaging environment where early-career journalists can interact with experienced reporters, editors, scholars, and thought leaders through meaningful conversations rooted in the Aspen Institute’s long-standing tradition of respectful dialogue and critical thinking.

The Michael Cromartie Forum is more than a journalism conference—it is an immersive learning experience that equips participants with new perspectives, stronger reporting skills, and valuable professional networks that can influence their careers for years to come.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and are expected to close around July 1, 2026. Early applications are strongly encouraged.

About the Michael Cromartie Forum

In today’s rapidly evolving media landscape, journalists are expected to cover increasingly complex issues that intersect with politics, religion, identity, ethics, democracy, and culture. Religion continues to influence elections, policymaking, international affairs, education, social movements, and community life, making religious literacy an essential skill for modern journalism.

Recognizing this need, the Aspen Institute’s Religion & Society Program established the Michael Cromartie Forum to help emerging journalists better understand how faith and religion influence public life while strengthening their ability to report accurately, fairly, and thoughtfully on these issues.

Named in honor of the late Michael Cromartie, a respected advocate for improving religion reporting in mainstream media, the Forum continues his legacy by creating space for journalists to engage with scholars and experienced practitioners in open, nonpartisan dialogue. The program emphasizes curiosity, intellectual humility, and rigorous discussion rather than debate or advocacy.

What Participants Can Expect

During the three-day Forum, participants will engage in a series of carefully curated discussions, presentations, and interactive sessions covering topics at the intersection of religion and public life.

The program brings together:

  • Experienced journalists from leading media organizations.
  • Editors with expertise in religion, politics, and public affairs reporting.
  • Distinguished scholars and researchers.
  • Public intellectuals and subject-matter experts.
  • Fellow emerging journalists from diverse backgrounds.

Rather than focusing on breaking news, the Forum encourages participants to examine the deeper historical, cultural, philosophical, and ethical dimensions of current events. This format allows journalists to gain insights that strengthen their long-term reporting and storytelling abilities.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the Michael Cromartie Forum offers numerous professional and personal benefits, including:

  • Exposure to leading scholars and experienced journalists.
  • A deeper understanding of religion’s influence on politics, society, and culture.
  • Opportunities to improve reporting on complex public issues.
  • Networking with fellow journalists from different media organizations.
  • Participation in thoughtful, nonpartisan discussions.
  • Membership in a growing community of journalism professionals committed to intellectual excellence.
  • Professional growth through meaningful dialogue and collaborative learning.

The Forum provides participants with the time and space to think critically about journalism beyond the pressures of daily newsroom deadlines.

Who Is the Forum Designed For?

The program is primarily intended for emerging journalists between the ages of 22 and 30, although the organizers clearly state that there are no strict age requirements.

Applicants may include:

  • Print journalists.
  • Broadcast journalists.
  • Digital media reporters.
  • Multimedia journalists.
  • Editors early in their careers.
  • Independent journalists.
  • Freelance reporters.
  • Journalism graduates beginning their professional careers.

The organizers welcome applicants who demonstrate intellectual curiosity, a commitment to high-quality journalism, and an interest in understanding the role religion plays in contemporary society.

Venue and Dates

The 2026 Michael Cromartie Forum will take place in:

Location: Middleburg, Virginia, USA

Dates: August 30 – September 1, 2026

The setting is intentionally designed to encourage focused learning, reflection, relationship-building, and open dialogue among participants.

Why Religion Reporting Matters

Religion remains one of the most influential forces shaping communities across the world. It affects public policy, elections, education, healthcare, international relations, social justice movements, humanitarian work, and cultural identity.

Despite this influence, religion is often misunderstood or underreported in mainstream journalism.

The Michael Cromartie Forum seeks to bridge this gap by helping journalists:

  1. Improve religious literacy.
  2. Better understand diverse faith traditions.
  3. Report accurately on religion-related issues.
  4. Explore how religious beliefs intersect with politics and public life.
  5. Produce more nuanced, balanced, and informed journalism.

These skills are increasingly valuable in today’s interconnected and multicultural world.

Professional Networking Opportunities

One of the Forum’s greatest strengths is the opportunity to build lasting professional relationships.

Participants become part of a network that includes:

  • National and international journalists.
  • Editors.
  • Academic experts.
  • Public policy specialists.
  • Religion scholars.
  • Media leaders.

These connections often lead to future collaborations, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the official application through the Aspen Institute Online Form.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning candidates may receive consideration as applications are submitted. Since spaces are limited, applying early significantly improves the chances of being considered.

Expected Application Deadline: Around July 1, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare a thoughtful application that demonstrates their journalism experience, curiosity about public affairs, and commitment to producing accurate, insightful reporting.

Discover more global conference opportunities on OFY here

The post Aspen Institute Michael Cromartie Forum 2026 for Emerging Journalists in the USA: Apply for a Transformative Journalism Experience appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026: Birregional Training for Women Leaders in Local Politics Across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The EU-LAC Foundation has officially launched the EU-LAC Birregional School of Local Political Leadership for Women 2026, a high-impact leadership development programme designed to strengthen women’s political participation, advocacy skills, and leadership capacity at the local level across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union. This initiative is a strategic space for training, exchange, […]

The post EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026: Birregional Training for Women Leaders in Local Politics Across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The EU-LAC Foundation has officially launched the EU-LAC Birregional School of Local Political Leadership for Women 2026, a high-impact leadership development programme designed to strengthen women’s political participation, advocacy skills, and leadership capacity at the local level across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union.

This initiative is a strategic space for training, exchange, and networking, bringing together women leaders from different political, community, and social backgrounds. The programme is built on a feminist, intersectional, and human rights-based approach, aiming to empower women to lead democratic, inclusive, and transformative change within their communities.

The School combines virtual preparatory sessions with a face-to-face intensive training in Panama, ensuring both accessibility and meaningful intercultural exchange among participants.

Objective of the EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School

The main goal of the programme is to strengthen the capacity of women engaged in territorial and local leadership by providing:

  • Advanced training in political leadership and governance
  • Practical tools for communication, negotiation, and public management
  • Opportunities for intergenerational mentoring
  • Strong birregional networking between Europe, Latin America, and the Caribbean
  • A platform for sharing lived experiences and community-based leadership strategies

Through this initiative, the EU-LAC Foundation aims to reinforce women’s leadership in democratic governance and civic participation at the local level.

 

Format of the Leadership School

The programme follows a blended learning structure, combining digital and in-person components:

  1. Virtual Preparatory Phase
  • Online training modules before the in-person session
  • Introduction to leadership concepts, tools, and frameworks
  • Networking and group discussions across regions
  1. In-Person Intensive Session
  • Held in Panama (October 2026)
  • High-level training workshops
  • Interactive leadership sessions
  • Cross-regional experience sharing
  • Networking with women leaders from EU-LAC regions
  1. Follow-Up Activities
  • Continued engagement through networks
  • Participation in the EU-LAC WIN Network
  • Knowledge sharing and leadership development continuation

 

Profile of Applicants

The programme is designed for two main categories of women leaders:

  1. Young Women Leaders

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Membership in a network, organisation, programme, or institution
  • Interest in territorial political leadership (formal or informal)
  • Engagement in civic participation, feminist groups, or community initiatives
  • Motivation to strengthen leadership skills and collaborate with women across regions
  • Interest in EU-LAC relations, gender equality, or local development
  • Willingness to join or engage with the EU-LAC WIN Network
  1. Senior Women Leaders

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong and consolidated leadership experience in political or community spaces
  • Proven impact in local governance, municipalities, councils, or civil society initiatives
  • Membership in a recognised institution, network, or organisation
  • Capacity to mentor and support younger women leaders
  • Commitment to feminist and intersectional leadership principles
  • Active contribution to community transformation and policy influence

 

Required Application Documents

All applications must be submitted in English or Spanish and include:

  1. Signed application form
  2. Proof of membership in a network, organisation, or institution
  3. Letter of endorsement or support from a relevant institution or organisation

Submission Format

  • PDF or MS Word format only
  • Incomplete or late applications will not be considered

 

Key Benefits of the Programme

Selected participants will gain access to a wide range of professional and personal development opportunities, including:

  • High-level leadership training in political and civic engagement
  • Strengthening negotiation, communication, and governance skills
  • Participation in a birregional leadership network
  • Exposure to feminist and intersectional leadership frameworks
  • Opportunities for mentorship and intergenerational learning
  • Exchange of experiences across Europe, Latin America, and the Caribbean
  • Capacity building in local governance and advocacy

Financial Support (Scholarships)

For selected participants who cannot self-finance their participation, the EU-LAC Foundation will provide:

  • International travel coverage
  • Hotel accommodation
  • Local transport in Panama
  • Daily stipend for meals and subsistence during the in-person phase

 

Application Deadline

Friday, 24 July 2026 (23:59 CEST – Germany time)

Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted.

 

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted by email to:

  • Email: eulacwin@eulacfoundation.org
  • Attention: Dr Anna Barrera
  • Subject Line: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 + Applicant’s Name

 

Selection Process & Communication

  • Applications will be reviewed by an internal selection committee
  • Evaluation period: approximately 3 weeks after the deadline
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted via email
  • Applicants are advised not to send repeated inquiries during the review period
  • All official updates will be communicated electronically

 

Final Overview

The EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 is a unique opportunity for women leaders to build transformative leadership skills, strengthen democratic participation, and join a powerful international network committed to gender equality and social change.

By combining training, mentorship, and intercultural dialogue, the programme empowers women to become stronger voices in local governance and community transformation.

 

Applications should be sent to the attention of Dr Anna Barrera (EU-LAC Foundation).
Email: eulacwin@eulacfoundation.org
Subject line: EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026 + Applicant’s Name

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post EU-LAC Women’s Political Leadership School 2026: Birregional Training for Women Leaders in Local Politics Across Latin America, the Caribbean, and the European Union appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) Opportunities at the University of Johannesburg: Global Excellence and Societal Impact (GES 4.0) Initiative
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The University of Johannesburg (UJ), through its Global Excellence and Stature (GES 4.0) Social Impact Initiative, is inviting applications from highly qualified and early-career researchers for its Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) programme.
