This prestigious fellowship is designed to attract outstanding postdoctoral researchers who can contribute meaningfully to high-impact research, innovation, interdisciplinarity, and societal transformation. This […]

The post Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) Opportunities at the University of Johannesburg: Global Excellence and Societal Impact (GES 4.0) Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Johannesburg (UJ), through its Global Excellence and Stature (GES 4.0) Social Impact Initiative, is inviting applications from highly qualified and early-career researchers for its Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) programme. This prestigious fellowship is designed to attract outstanding postdoctoral researchers who can contribute meaningfully to high-impact research, innovation, interdisciplinarity, and societal transformation.

This fellowship is part of UJ’s long-term strategic vision (UJ Strategic Plan 2035), which positions the university as a globally competitive research institution addressing critical challenges facing society, the environment, governance systems, and technological transformation.

The programme seeks exceptional scholars who demonstrate strong academic excellence, innovative thinking, and a commitment to solving real-world problems aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

 

Overview of the Postdoctoral Research Fellowship Programme

The PDRF at the University of Johannesburg is a full-time research fellowship that supports emerging scholars in conducting advanced research across priority thematic areas. Fellows will be embedded within recognized research entities, centres, institutes, SARChI Chairs, or strategic research programmes at UJ.

This ensures that successful applicants are fully integrated into a vibrant academic environment that promotes collaboration, mentorship, and interdisciplinary research output.

 

Priority Research Areas

Applicants must align their research proposals with one or more of the following thematic areas:

  • Sustainability, Climate Change, and Environmental Governance (ESG)
  • Water, Energy, Food, and Environmental Security
  • Public Health and Mental Health Systems
  • Education and Child Development
  • Migration, Social Justice, and Inequality
  • Governance, Democracy, and Public Policy
  • African Futures and Pan-African Thought
  • Advanced Manufacturing and Industry 4.0
  • Smart Systems and Data Science
  • Innovation, Entrepreneurship, and Societal Impact
  • Artificial Intelligence and Digital Transformation

These areas reflect UJ’s commitment to addressing global and African developmental challenges through cutting-edge research.

 

Fellowship Conditions and Benefits

Successful applicants will receive a highly competitive research support package designed to enable impactful academic work.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Competitive tax-free stipend of R250,000 per year
  • Initial appointment for 12 months, renewable based on performance and funding availability
  • Access to world-class UJ research infrastructure
  • Integration into national and international research networks
  • Mentorship from senior academics and research leaders
  • Opportunities for professional development and academic growth

The fellowship does not include relocation or accommodation support.

 

Expectations of Postdoctoral Fellows

Fellows are expected to contribute actively to the academic and research environment at UJ. Responsibilities include:

  • Participation in capacity-building and research activities
  • Co-supervision of postgraduate students
  • Development of research funding proposals
  • Presentation of research findings at conferences, seminars, and webinars
  • Publishing research in peer-reviewed journals, books, or scholarly platforms
  • Active participation in collaborative research networks
  • Contribution to additional research initiatives aligned with host entities

This ensures fellows not only conduct research but also contribute to the broader academic ecosystem.

 

Eligibility and Minimum Requirements

Applicants must meet the following criteria:

Academic Requirements

  • Hold a PhD or equivalent doctoral qualification (NQF Level 10)
  • PhD must have been obtained within the last five years
  • Must not have held a previous postdoctoral fellowship at the host discipline (preferred condition)

Research Competencies

  • Strong scholarly and research performance record
  • Excellent academic writing and communication skills
  • Proven research output or publications

Other Requirements

  • Ability to work full-time for the duration of the fellowship
  • Demonstrated interest in interdisciplinary research and societal impact
  • Preference for candidates with international collaboration experience

 

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a complete application package including:

  1. Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  2. Certified copies of academic qualifications
  3. Copy of doctoral thesis abstract
  4. Motivation letter outlining suitability for the fellowship
  5. Research proposal (3–5 pages) aligned to a host research area
  6. Two academic reference letters
  7. Evidence of publications or research outputs

 

Application Process

Applications must be submitted electronically to the designated application platform or contact person provided by the university.

For enquiries:

  • Contact: Mr Thabiso Masango
  • Email: thabisom@uj.ac.za

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

 

Value of the Fellowship

The PDRF at UJ is not only a financial opportunity but also a career-defining academic experience. It provides:

  • High-level research exposure in a globally recognized institution
  • Access to interdisciplinary academic networks
  • Opportunities to shape policy-relevant and impactful research
  • Strong foundation for future academic or research leadership roles

 

Application Deadline

15 July 2026

 

Conclusion

The University of Johannesburg Postdoctoral Research Fellowship under the GES 4.0 Initiative represents one of the most competitive research opportunities in Africa. It is designed for emerging scholars who are ready to contribute to global knowledge production while addressing pressing societal challenges.

Applicants with strong academic backgrounds, innovative research ideas, and interdisciplinary interests are strongly encouraged to apply.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Postdoctoral Research Fellowship (PDRF) Opportunities at the University of Johannesburg: Global Excellence and Societal Impact (GES 4.0) Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
